\paragraph{Qualification, three ways.} Figure~\ref{fig:groupqual} is the pre-kickoff (Frozen) qualification decomposition --- win the group, finish second, or advance as a best third --- locked and unchanged. The two figures below recompute it after 54 results: Track~A conditions on the played outcomes with the June~10 ratings held fixed, and Track~B additionally folds in live Elo and bookmaker odds. Conditioning sharpens every group that has kicked off --- a win lifts a team's qualification mass and a defeat cuts it --- while groups still to play stay at the Frozen baseline. The largest swing so far is Ghana, up from 22.9\% under Frozen to 100.0\% under Track~A.

\begin{figure}[!t]
  \caption{Qualification probability by team --- Track~A (conditioned on \liveDocumented{} results, June~10 ratings frozen; live edition M\liveEditionNum{})}\label{fig:live_groupqual}
  {\centering\includegraphics[width=0.94\textwidth]{figs/fig_live_groupqual.pdf}\par}
\end{figure}

\begin{figure}[!t]
  \caption{Qualification probability by team --- Track~B (result-conditioning + live Elo + bookmaker odds; live edition M\liveEditionNum{})}\label{fig:live_groupqual_b}
  {\centering\includegraphics[width=0.94\textwidth]{figs/fig_live_groupqual_b.pdf}\par}
\end{figure}
